IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShaderInput

Sets the byte offset from the start of the vertex buffer data. This function can work with push-buffer fix-ups.

HRESULT SetVertexShaderInput(
  DWORD Handle,
  UINT StreamCount,
  CONST D3DSTREAM_INPUT *pStreamInputs
);

Parameters

Handle
[in] Valid handle to a vertex shader whose input declaration is used to program the GPU's stream registers. In this case, any vertex shader constant values declared in the declaration are ignored. The handle cannot be a flexible vertex format (FVF).
StreamCount
[in] Number of elements in the array pointed to by pStreamInputs.
pStreamInputs
[in] Pointer to an array of D3DSTREAM_INPUT structures used to specify vertex buffers and the offsets from the starts of those vertex buffers.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is S_OK.

Remarks

Calling SetVertexShaderInput does not set a vertex shader, it only sets the format that a vertex shader is expecting. The GPU is not set to use a vertex shader until SetVertexShader, SelectVertexShader, or SelectVertexShaderDirect is called.

When IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShaderInput is called with a non-NULL Handle, IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShaderInput takes control of the GPU's stream registers until IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShaderInput is called with a NULL Handle. This means that after calling SetVertexShaderInput, any future calls to SelectVertexShader or SelectVertexShaderDirect will not change the vertex attribute format and calls to SetStreamSource will not set the stream inputs. After a call of SetVertexShaderInput(NULL, 0, NULL), those functions will once again take effect.

This method doesn't support the notion of a non-zero IndexBase value.

The D3DTSS_TEXCOORDINDEX mapping for every texture stage must be identity.

The function IDirect3DDevice8::GetVertexShaderInput may be use used to query the set value.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.

See Also

IDirect3DDevice8::GetVertexShaderInput
